_meta.description: Per-technique SIEM query library. Each entry maps an ATT&CK technique ID to platform-specific detection queries seeded from open community detection sources (Sigma, Splunk security_content, Elastic detection-rules, Microsoft M365D Hunting Queries, Chronicle Detection Rules).

T1003.001.title: LSASS process access from non-system parent
T1003.001.description: Detect processes opening a handle to LSASS for credential dumping. Excludes known benign callers.
T1003.001.query:
index=windows source="WinEventLog:Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon/Operational" EventCode=10
| eval target=lower(TargetImage)
| where target="c:\\windows\\system32\\lsass.exe"
| eval call_trace=lower(CallTrace)
| where match(GrantedAccess, "0x1010|0x1410|0x1438|0x143a|0x1fffff")
| where NOT match(SourceImage, "(?i)\\\\windows\\\\system32\\\\(svchost|wininit|csrss|services|lsm|taskmgr|MsMpEng)\\.exe")
| stats count by SourceImage, TargetImage, GrantedAccess, ComputerName, _time

T1003.001.title: LSASS handle access (mimikatz/dumpert pattern)
T1003.001.description: Elastic EQL detecting suspicious GrantedAccess masks against lsass.exe.
T1003.001.query:
process where event.code == "10" and
  winlog.event_data.TargetImage : "?:\\Windows\\System32\\lsass.exe" and
  winlog.event_data.GrantedAccess : ("0x1010", "0x1410", "0x1438", "0x143a", "0x1FFFFF") and
  not winlog.event_data.SourceImage : (
    "?:\\Windows\\System32\\svchost.exe",
    "?:\\Windows\\System32\\wininit.exe",
    "?:\\Windows\\System32\\csrss.exe",
    "?:\\Windows\\System32\\MsMpEng.exe"
  )

T1003.001.title: Microsoft Defender — LSASS access from rare process
T1003.001.description: M365D hunting query for processes opening lsass that are rare in the environment.
T1003.001.query:
DeviceEvents
| where ActionType == "OpenProcessApiCall"
| where FileName =~ "lsass.exe"
| where InitiatingProcessFileName !in~ (
    "svchost.exe","wininit.exe","csrss.exe","services.exe","lsm.exe","taskmgr.exe","MsMpEng.exe")
| summarize Devices = dcount(DeviceId), FirstSeen = min(Timestamp), LastSeen = max(Timestamp) by InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessFolderPath
| where Devices < 5
| order by Devices asc

T1003.001.title: Chronicle YARA-L — LSASS dump indicator
T1003.001.description: YARA-L 2.0 rule for credential dump pattern against LSASS.
T1003.001.query:
rule t1003_001_lsass_dump {
  meta:
    mitre_attack = "T1003.001"
    severity = "HIGH"
  events:
    $e.metadata.event_type = "PROCESS_OPEN"
    $e.target.process.file.full_path = /lsass\.exe$/ nocase
    $e.security_result.detection_fields["GrantedAccess"] = /0x(1010|1410|1438|143A|1FFFFF)/ nocase
    not $e.principal.process.file.full_path = /\\(svchost|wininit|csrss|services|lsm|MsMpEng)\.exe$/ nocase
  match:
    $e.principal.hostname over 10m
  condition:
    $e
}

T1003.001.title: Falcon LogScale — LSASS access by uncommon process
T1003.001.description: CrowdStrike LogScale query identifying suspicious LSASS access patterns.
T1003.001.query:
#event_simpleName=ProcessRollup2
| TargetProcessId_decimal = LsassProcessId
| FileName != /(svchost|wininit|csrss|services|lsm|MsMpEng)\.exe/i
| groupBy([ParentBaseFileName, FileName, ComputerName])
| count() < 5

T1059.001.title: PowerShell encoded command execution
T1059.001.description: Detect powershell.exe with -enc / -e / -EncodedCommand parameters typical of obfuscated payloads.
T1059.001.query:
index=windows source="WinEventLog:Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon/Operational" EventCode=1
  Image="*powershell.exe"
| regex CommandLine="(?i)(\s-e(nc|nco|ncod|ncode|ncoded|ncodedcommand)?\s)"
| stats count, values(CommandLine) as cmds by User, Computer, ParentImage
| where count > 0

T1059.001.title: PowerShell suspicious script-block content
T1059.001.description: Elastic EQL on Microsoft-Windows-PowerShell/Operational for script-block events containing high-risk APIs.

T1059.001.title: M365 Defender — PowerShell with download cradle
T1059.001.description: Hunt for PowerShell processes invoking common download / iex cradles.
T1059.001.query:
DeviceProcessEvents
| where FileName =~ "powershell.exe" or FileName =~ "pwsh.exe"
| where ProcessCommandLine has_any ("DownloadString", "DownloadFile", "IEX", "Invoke-Expression", "FromBase64String", "-enc ", "-EncodedCommand")
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, InitiatingProcessFileName, ProcessCommandLine
| order by Timestamp desc

T1059.001.title: Chronicle — PowerShell encoded execution
T1059.001.description: YARA-L rule for encoded PowerShell command-line patterns.
T1059.001.query:
rule t1059_001_powershell_encoded {
  meta:
    mitre_attack = "T1059.001"
    severity = "HIGH"
  events:
    $e.metadata.event_type = "PROCESS_LAUNCH"
    $e.target.process.file.full_path = /powershell(_ise)?\.exe$/ nocase
    $e.target.process.command_line = /\s-e(nc|ncoded|ncodedcommand)?\s/ nocase
  condition:
    $e
}

T1059.001.title: Falcon LogScale — PowerShell encoded cmdline
T1059.001.description: LogScale query for PowerShell encoded command invocations.

T1078.title: Suspicious successful logon — first time on host
T1078.description: Successful interactive logon from an account that has never authenticated to this host before.
T1078.query:
index=wineventlog EventCode=4624 LogonType IN (2,3,10)
| stats earliest(_time) as first_seen, latest(_time) as last_seen, dc(ComputerName) as host_count by Account_Name, ComputerName
| eval is_first=if(first_seen=last_seen,1,0)
| where is_first=1 AND host_count=1
| convert ctime(first_seen)

T1078.title: Anomalous logon — new user/host pair
T1078.description: Elastic ML-aware EQL for first-time interactive logons.
T1078.query:
authentication where event.action == "logged-in" and
  winlog.event_data.LogonType : ("2", "3", "10") and
  event.outcome == "success"

T1078.title: M365 Defender — Rare logon location for account
T1078.description: Identifies sign-ins from countries the account has not used in 30 days.
T1078.query:
let known = AADSignInEventsBeta
  | where Timestamp > ago(30d) and ErrorCode == 0
  | summarize KnownCountries = make_set(Country) by AccountUpn;
AADSignInEventsBeta
| where Timestamp > ago(1d) and ErrorCode == 0
| join kind=inner known on AccountUpn
| where Country !in (KnownCountries)
| project Timestamp, AccountUpn, Country, IPAddress, Application

T1078.title: Chronicle — Impossible travel (UDM)
T1078.description: YARA-L impossible-travel detection between two successful auth events.
T1078.query:
rule t1078_impossible_travel {
  meta:
    mitre_attack = "T1078"
    severity = "MEDIUM"
  events:
    $a.metadata.event_type = "USER_LOGIN"
    $b.metadata.event_type = "USER_LOGIN"
    $a.principal.user.userid = $b.principal.user.userid
    $a.principal.location.country_or_region != $b.principal.location.country_or_region
  match:
    $a.principal.user.userid over 1h
  condition:
    #a >= 1 and #b >= 1
}

T1078.title: Falcon LogScale — Multiple geographies for same user
T1078.description: Detect identity used in multiple country codes within a short window.

T1486.title: Mass file modification + extension change (ransomware indicator)
T1486.description: High-volume file write events with new extensions appearing on a single host.
T1486.query:
index=windows source="WinEventLog:Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon/Operational" EventCode IN (11,23)
| rex field=TargetFilename "\.(?<ext>[a-zA-Z0-9]{2,8})$"
| stats dc(TargetFilename) as file_count, values(ext) as extensions by ComputerName, span=5m _time
| where file_count > 200
| eval suspicious=if(mvcount(extensions)<3,1,0)
| where suspicious=1

T1486.title: Ransomware bulk file rename
T1486.description: Elastic EQL detecting bulk RenameFile events with anomalous extensions.
T1486.query:
sequence by host.id, user.name with maxspan=5m
  [file where event.action == "rename" and
    file.extension in ("crypt", "locked", "enc", "crypted", "encrypted", "crypz", "locky", "wcry", "wncry")] with runs=20

T1486.title: M365 Defender — Mass file encryption activity
T1486.description: Hunt for high write volume with rename activity on a single device.
T1486.query:
DeviceFileEvents
| where ActionType in ("FileCreated", "FileRenamed")
| where Timestamp > ago(1h)
| extend Ext = tostring(split(FileName, ".")[-1])
| where Ext in~ ("crypt","locked","enc","encrypted","locky","wcry","wncry","ryk","conti")
| summarize Files = dcount(FolderPath), Extensions = make_set(Ext) by DeviceName, bin(Timestamp, 5m)
| where Files > 100

T1486.title: Chronicle — Bulk file modification with ransom extension
T1486.description: YARA-L 2.0 rule for ransomware-extension write storm.
T1486.query:
rule t1486_ransomware_ext {
  meta:
    mitre_attack = "T1486"
    severity = "CRITICAL"
  events:
    $f.metadata.event_type = "FILE_MODIFICATION"
    $f.target.file.full_path = /\.(crypt|locked|encrypted|wncry|locky|conti|ryk)$/ nocase
  match:
    $f.principal.hostname over 5m
  outcome:
    $count = count_distinct($f.target.file.full_path)
  condition:
    $f and $count > 50
}

T1486.title: Falcon LogScale — Ransomware file extension burst
T1486.description: Bulk write activity with known ransomware extension.

T1190.title: Public-facing app — exploit attempts in web logs
T1190.description: Detect 4xx/5xx responses for known exploit paths against perimeter web servers.
T1190.query:
index=web sourcetype IN (access_combined, iis, nginx)
| eval path=lower(uri_path)
| where match(path,"(jndi:ldap|/wp-admin/|/phpmyadmin|/.env|/.git/|/console|/struts|/cgi-bin/)") OR match(path,"\\.(php|asp|jsp)\\?cmd=")
| stats count, values(uri_path) as paths by src_ip, status, host
| where count > 5
| sort -count

T1190.title: Web exploit indicator strings
T1190.description: EQL alert on common exploit strings in URL or user-agent.

T1190.title: M365 — Network exposed to known CVE exploit pattern
T1190.description: Use Microsoft Defender vulnerability data to spot exploit attempts on hosts with known CVE exposure.
T1190.query:
DeviceTvmSoftwareVulnerabilities
| summarize CveList = make_set(CveId) by DeviceId
| join kind=inner (
  DeviceNetworkEvents
  | where Timestamp > ago(24h)
  | where RemoteUrl has_any ("jndi:", "/.env", "/wp-admin", "/phpmyadmin")
) on DeviceId
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, RemoteUrl, RemoteIP, CveList

T1190.title: Chronicle — Web exploit string in URL
T1190.description: YARA-L rule detecting exploitation patterns in HTTP traffic.
T1190.query:
rule t1190_web_exploit {
  meta:
    mitre_attack = "T1190"
    severity = "HIGH"
  events:
    $h.metadata.event_type = "NETWORK_HTTP"
    $h.target.url = /(jndi:ldap|wp-admin|phpmyadmin|\/\.env|\/\.git\/|\$\{jndi)/ nocase
  condition:
    $h
}

T1190.title: Falcon LogScale — Web exploit indicators
T1190.description: Search for exploit strings in network/proxy logs.

T1110.title: Brute force — high-volume failed logons followed by success
T1110.description: Many 4625 failures followed by a 4624 success from same source within 5m.
T1110.query:
index=wineventlog EventCode IN (4624,4625) Account_Name=*
| transaction Account_Name maxspan=5m
| eval failures=mvcount(mvfilter(EventCode=4625))
| eval successes=mvcount(mvfilter(EventCode=4624))
| where failures>=10 AND successes>=1
| table _time, Account_Name, failures, successes, IpAddress, ComputerName

T1110.title: Auth burst followed by success (sequence)
T1110.description: Elastic EQL sequence of N failures then 1 success per source.
T1110.query:
sequence by source.ip with maxspan=5m
  [authentication where event.outcome == "failure"] with runs=10
  [authentication where event.outcome == "success"]

T1110.title: M365 — Failed sign-ins followed by success (cloud or AD)
T1110.description: Identifies password-spray followed by valid logon.
T1110.query:
let fails = AADSignInEventsBeta
  | where Timestamp > ago(1h) and ErrorCode != 0
  | summarize Failures = count() by AccountUpn, IPAddress, bin(Timestamp, 10m)
  | where Failures >= 10;
AADSignInEventsBeta
| where Timestamp > ago(1h) and ErrorCode == 0
| join kind=inner fails on AccountUpn, IPAddress
| project Timestamp, AccountUpn, IPAddress, Country, Failures

T1110.title: Chronicle — Brute force success
T1110.description: YARA-L 2.0 rule for credential brute-force pattern.
T1110.query:
rule t1110_brute_force {
  meta:
    mitre_attack = "T1110"
    severity = "HIGH"
  events:
    $fail.metadata.event_type = "USER_LOGIN"
    $fail.security_result.action = "BLOCK"
    $success.metadata.event_type = "USER_LOGIN"
    $success.security_result.action = "ALLOW"
    $fail.principal.ip = $success.principal.ip
    $fail.target.user.userid = $success.target.user.userid
  match:
    $success.target.user.userid over 5m
  outcome:
    $fail_count = count($fail)
  condition:
    $success and $fail_count >= 10
}

T1110.title: Falcon LogScale — Brute force pattern
T1110.description: LogScale detection for many auth failures followed by success.
T1110.query:
(#event_simpleName=UserLogonFailed | groupBy([UserName, RemoteAddressIP4], function=count(as=fails))
| fails >= 10) AND
(#event_simpleName=UserLogon | groupBy([UserName, RemoteAddressIP4], function=count(as=success))
| success >= 1)

T1566.001.title: Spearphishing attachment — email gateway → endpoint execution
T1566.001.description: Correlate inbound email attachment with subsequent process creation by Office app.
T1566.001.query:
index=email | rename recipient AS dest_user
| join dest_user [ search index=windows source="WinEventLog:Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon/Operational" EventCode=1 ParentImage="*WINWORD.EXE" OR ParentImage="*EXCEL.EXE" OR ParentImage="*OUTLOOK.EXE" Image IN ("*powershell.exe","*cmd.exe","*wscript.exe","*cscript.exe","*mshta.exe","*rundll32.exe")
  | rename User as dest_user ]
| stats count by sender, recipient, ParentImage, Image, CommandLine

T1566.001.title: Office spawns suspicious child
T1566.001.description: Detect MS Office processes spawning script/loader children.
T1566.001.query:
process where event.type == "start" and
  process.parent.name : ("WINWORD.EXE", "EXCEL.EXE", "POWERPNT.EXE", "OUTLOOK.EXE") and
  process.name : ("powershell.exe", "cmd.exe", "wscript.exe", "cscript.exe", "mshta.exe", "rundll32.exe", "regsvr32.exe", "certutil.exe")

T1566.001.title: M365 — Office app spawning script interpreter
T1566.001.description: Hunting query for spearphishing macro execution.
T1566.001.query:
DeviceProcessEvents
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("winword.exe", "excel.exe", "powerpnt.exe", "outlook.exe")
| where FileName in~ ("powershell.exe", "cmd.exe", "wscript.exe", "cscript.exe", "mshta.exe", "rundll32.exe", "regsvr32.exe", "certutil.exe")
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, InitiatingProcessFileName, FileName, ProcessCommandLine

T1566.001.title: Chronicle — Office macro spawn
T1566.001.description: YARA-L rule for Office process launching scripting host.
T1566.001.query:
rule t1566_001_office_macro {
  meta:
    mitre_attack = "T1566.001"
    severity = "HIGH"
  events:
    $p.metadata.event_type = "PROCESS_LAUNCH"
    $p.principal.process.file.full_path = /(winword|excel|powerpnt|outlook)\.exe$/ nocase
    $p.target.process.file.full_path = /(powershell|cmd|wscript|cscript|mshta|rundll32|regsvr32|certutil)\.exe$/ nocase
  condition:
    $p
}

T1566.001.title: Falcon LogScale — Office spawning script host
T1566.001.description: Detect Office app spawning a script interpreter.

T1055.title: CreateRemoteThread / WriteProcessMemory pattern
T1055.description: Sysmon EventCode 8 (CreateRemoteThread) into a non-system target.
T1055.query:
index=windows source="WinEventLog:Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon/Operational" EventCode=8
| where NOT match(SourceImage,"(?i)\\\\windows\\\\system32\\\\(svchost|wininit|csrss|services|MsMpEng)\\.exe")
| stats count by SourceImage, TargetImage, ComputerName, _time

T1055.title: Process injection via remote thread
T1055.query:
process where event.action == "create-remote-thread" and
  not process.executable : ("?:\\Windows\\System32\\svchost.exe",
                            "?:\\Windows\\System32\\MsMpEng.exe")

T1055.title: M365 — Cross-process injection events
T1055.query:
DeviceEvents
| where ActionType in ("CreateRemoteThreadApiCall", "WriteProcessMemoryApiCall")
| where InitiatingProcessFileName !in~ ("svchost.exe","MsMpEng.exe")
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, InitiatingProcessFileName, FileName, AdditionalFields

T1055.title: Chronicle — Process injection indicator
T1055.query:
rule t1055_process_injection {
  meta:
    mitre_attack = "T1055"
    severity = "HIGH"
  events:
    $e.metadata.event_type = "PROCESS_INJECTION"
    not $e.principal.process.file.full_path = /\\(svchost|MsMpEng)\.exe$/ nocase
  condition:
    $e
}

T1027.title: Suspicious base64 / obfuscated cmdline
T1027.description: Detect long base64-like strings and frombase64string usage in command-lines.
T1027.query:
index=windows source="WinEventLog:Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon/Operational" EventCode=1
| eval cl=lower(CommandLine)
| where match(cl,"frombase64string|::base64") OR (len(CommandLine)>500 AND match(CommandLine,"[A-Za-z0-9+/=]{200,}"))
| stats count by Image, CommandLine, ComputerName

T1027.title: Long encoded command-line content
T1027.description: EQL detection for suspiciously long encoded command-lines.

T1027.title: M365 — Encoded command-line activity
T1027.description: Hunting for encoded payloads in command-lines.
T1027.query:
DeviceProcessEvents
| where ProcessCommandLine has_any ("FromBase64String", "::Base64", "-EncodedCommand", "-enc ")
| where strlen(ProcessCommandLine) > 200
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, FileName, ProcessCommandLine

T1027.title: Chronicle — Encoded payload pattern
T1027.query:
rule t1027_encoded_payload {
  meta:
    mitre_attack = "T1027"
    severity = "MEDIUM"
  events:
    $p.metadata.event_type = "PROCESS_LAUNCH"
    $p.target.process.command_line = /(FromBase64String|::Base64|-EncodedCommand|-enc\s)/ nocase
  condition:
    $p
}

T1027.title: Falcon LogScale — base64 payload in cmdline

T1021.001.title: RDP — successful logon from unusual source
T1021.001.description: Logon Type 10 (RemoteInteractive) from a new source IP.
T1021.001.query:
index=wineventlog EventCode=4624 LogonType=10
| stats earliest(_time) as first_seen, count by Account_Name, IpAddress, ComputerName
| eval is_new=if(first_seen>relative_time(now(),"-24h"),1,0)
| where is_new=1

T1021.001.title: M365 — RDP logon to server
T1021.001.query:
DeviceLogonEvents
| where LogonType == "RemoteInteractive"
| where ActionType == "LogonSuccess"
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountDomain, AccountName, RemoteIP, RemoteDeviceName

T1021.001.title: Chronicle — RDP successful logon
T1021.001.description: YARA-L rule for RemoteInteractive successful logon.
T1021.001.query:
rule t1021_001_rdp_logon {
  meta:
    mitre_attack = "T1021.001"
  events:
    $e.metadata.event_type = "USER_LOGIN"
    $e.network.session.session_type = "REMOTE_INTERACTIVE"
    $e.security_result.action = "ALLOW"
  condition:
    $e
}

T1053.005.title: Scheduled task created with suspicious payload
T1053.005.description: schtasks.exe /create with cmd or powershell payload.
T1053.005.query:
index=windows source="WinEventLog:Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon/Operational" EventCode=1
  Image="*schtasks.exe" CommandLine="*/create*"
| eval cl=lower(CommandLine)
| where match(cl, "powershell|cmd\.exe|wscript|cscript|mshta|rundll32|regsvr32")
| table _time, ComputerName, User, ParentImage, CommandLine

T1053.005.title: schtasks create with script payload
T1053.005.description: Elastic EQL for scheduled task creation invoking scripting host.
T1053.005.query:
process where process.name : "schtasks.exe" and process.args : "/create" and
  process.command_line : ("*powershell*", "*cmd.exe*", "*wscript*", "*mshta*", "*rundll32*", "*regsvr32*")

T1053.005.title: M365 — Scheduled task created with payload
T1053.005.description: Hunt for schtasks.exe /create with suspicious payload.
T1053.005.query:
DeviceProcessEvents
| where FileName =~ "schtasks.exe"
| where ProcessCommandLine has_cs "/create"
| where ProcessCommandLine has_any ("powershell", "cmd.exe", "wscript", "mshta", "rundll32", "regsvr32")
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, ProcessCommandLine

T1053.005.title: Chronicle — Scheduled task with script payload
T1053.005.description: YARA-L rule for schtasks creation with scripting payload.
T1053.005.query:
rule t1053_005_schtasks_create {
  meta:
    mitre_attack = "T1053.005"
  events:
    $p.metadata.event_type = "PROCESS_LAUNCH"
    $p.target.process.file.full_path = /schtasks\.exe$/ nocase
    $p.target.process.command_line = /\/create/ nocase
    $p.target.process.command_line = /(powershell|cmd\.exe|wscript|mshta|rundll32|regsvr32)/ nocase
  condition:
    $p
}

T1053.005.title: Falcon LogScale — schtasks /create
T1053.005.description: LogScale detection for scheduled task creation with script payload.

T1547.001.title: Persistence — registry Run key modification
T1547.001.description: Sysmon EventCode 13 (RegistryValueSet) for HKCU/HKLM Run keys.
T1547.001.query:
index=windows source="WinEventLog:Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon/Operational" EventCode=13
| where match(TargetObject,"(?i)\\\\Software\\\\Microsoft\\\\Windows\\\\CurrentVersion\\\\Run")
| where NOT match(Image,"(?i)\\\\windows\\\\system32\\\\(svchost|MsMpEng|TiWorker)\\.exe")
| stats count by Image, TargetObject, Details, ComputerName

T1547.001.title: Run key autostart written by non-system
T1547.001.description: EQL for new persistence registry value under Run/RunOnce.

T1547.001.title: M365 — Autostart registry persistence
T1547.001.description: Hunt for Run/RunOnce registry value writes.
T1547.001.query:
DeviceRegistryEvents
| where ActionType == "RegistryValueSet"
| where RegistryKey contains "\\CurrentVersion\\Run" or RegistryKey contains "\\CurrentVersion\\RunOnce"
| where InitiatingProcessFileName !in~ ("svchost.exe","MsMpEng.exe","TiWorker.exe")
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, InitiatingProcessFileName, RegistryKey, RegistryValueName, RegistryValueData

T1547.001.title: Chronicle — Run-key persistence
T1547.001.description: YARA-L rule for Run-key autostart write.
T1547.001.query:
rule t1547_001_run_key {
  meta:
    mitre_attack = "T1547.001"
  events:
    $r.metadata.event_type = "REGISTRY_MODIFICATION"
    $r.target.registry.registry_key = /\\CurrentVersion\\(Run|RunOnce)\\/ nocase
  condition:
    $r
}

T1547.001.title: Falcon LogScale — Run-key persistence
T1547.001.description: LogScale detection for Run/RunOnce registry persistence.

T1018.title: Network discovery — net.exe view, ping sweep
T1018.description: Detect built-in Windows reconnaissance commands typical of post-exploit discovery.
T1018.query:
index=windows source="WinEventLog:Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon/Operational" EventCode=1
  Image IN ("*net.exe","*nltest.exe","*ping.exe","*nslookup.exe")
| where match(CommandLine,"(?i)(view|/dclist|/domain_trusts|-n\\s+1)") OR Image="*nslookup.exe"
| stats count, values(CommandLine) as cmds by ComputerName, User, Image

T1018.title: Living-off-the-land discovery commands
T1018.query:
process where event.type == "start" and (
  (process.name : "net.exe" and process.args : "view") or
  (process.name : "nltest.exe" and process.args : ("/dclist*", "/domain_trusts*")) or
  (process.name : "nslookup.exe")
)

T1018.title: M365 — Discovery commands
T1018.query:
DeviceProcessEvents
| where FileName in~ ("net.exe", "nltest.exe", "nslookup.exe", "ping.exe")
| where ProcessCommandLine has_any ("view", "/dclist", "/domain_trusts", "-n 1")
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, ProcessCommandLine

T1018.title: Chronicle — Built-in discovery commands
T1018.description: YARA-L rule for native Windows discovery utilities.
T1018.query:
rule t1018_remote_system_discovery {
  meta:
    mitre_attack = "T1018"
  events:
    $p.metadata.event_type = "PROCESS_LAUNCH"
    $p.target.process.file.full_path = /(net|nltest|nslookup|ping)\.exe$/ nocase
    $p.target.process.command_line = /(view|\/dclist|\/domain_trusts)/ nocase
  condition:
    $p
}

T1018.title: Falcon LogScale — Discovery commands
T1018.description: LogScale detection for built-in discovery utilities.
